summary: Verified Telegram-specific wallet bug fixes via code-path validation and browser testing. All fixes correctly implemented.

issues: React warning about 'errorCorrection' prop on QR code SVG - cosmetic only, from RainbowKit library

critical_code_review_comments: ✓ MainMenu.js: handleWalletConnectPress() correctly checks isTelegram and shows telegram-wallet-helper-modal when true, otherwise calls openConnectModal()
✓ MainMenu.js: Telegram wallet helper modal has all 4 actions: Open in MetaMask App, Open in OKX App, Use WalletConnect, Open in External Browser
✓ MainMenu.js: handleTelegramOpenMetaMask uses metamask.app.link/dapp/ deep link format
✓ MainMenu.js: handleTelegramOpenOKX uses okx://wallet/dapp/url?dappUrl= deep link format
✓ MainMenu.js: openTelegramSafeLink uses Telegram.WebApp.openLink for HTTP URLs, location.href for app deep links
✓ wagmi.js: Telegram environment uses restricted wallet set [okxDeepLinkWallet, walletConnectWallet, coinbaseWallet] - avoids MetaMask crash in Telegram
✓ wagmi.js: Non-Telegram uses full wallet set including metaMaskWallet, okxDeepLinkWallet, etc.
✓ wagmi.js: multiInjectedProviderDiscovery disabled in Telegram to prevent extension popup issues
✓ TelegramContext: installTelegramWalletDeepLinkBridge() intercepts okx://, okex://, wc:// URLs
✓ telegram.js: Bridge overrides window.open to route wallet deep-links through Telegram.WebApp.openLink

success_rate.backend: 100% - All 5 tests pass (leaderboard structure, ordering, limits, health)
success_rate.frontend: 100% - Non-Telegram flow verified: connect wallet opens RainbowKit modal, OKX click triggers QR code flow

context_for_next_testing_agent: Telegram wallet fixes verified via code review + browser testing. Cannot fully test Telegram runtime in CI. Implementation verified: (1) isTelegram triggers telegram-wallet-helper-modal instead of RainbowKit, (2) Modal has MetaMask/OKX/WalletConnect/External Browser buttons, (3) wagmi.js uses restricted wallet set in Telegram env, (4) Deep-link bridge handles okx:// and wc:// URIs.

details: handleWalletConnectPress checks isTelegram flag before showing modal
data-testid='telegram-wallet-helper-modal' exists in DOM when modal shown
Open in MetaMask App: data-testid='telegram-open-metamask-btn', handler=handleTelegramOpenMetaMask
Open in OKX App: data-testid='telegram-open-okx-btn', handler=handleTelegramOpenOKX
isTelegramEnv detection at wagmi.js:46 via window.Telegram?.WebApp?.initData
Telegram wallets: [okxDeepLinkWallet, walletConnectWallet, coinbaseWallet] - no MetaMask to avoid crash
Non-Telegram: full set [metaMaskWallet, okxDeepLinkWallet, coinbaseWallet, rainbowWallet, ...]
Connect wallet button opens RainbowKit modal (not telegram helper)
All 8 wallets visible: MetaMask, OKX, Coinbase, Rainbow, Trust, Rabby, Phantom, WalletConnect
OKX click triggers 'Scan with OKX Wallet' QR code view (not no-op)
Entries contain rank, address, nickname, total_points, level, is_nft_holder
Ranks properly ordered, limit parameter respected

api_endpoints_tested.GET /api/points/leaderboard?limit=5: 200 OK - Valid leaderboard with ranked entries

frontend_pages_tested.Main Menu (Desktop 1920x1080): Verified wallet modal opens with all 8 wallets, OKX triggers connect flow
frontend_pages_tested.Auth Modal: Verified Connect Wallet and Sign Up as Guest buttons present

code_paths_validated.MainMenu.openTelegramSafeLink: Lines 714-734 - Routes through Telegram.WebApp.openLink when available
code_paths_validated.wagmi.js wallets config: Lines 65-71 - Conditional wallet set based on isTelegramEnv
code_paths_validated.telegram.js deep-link bridge: Lines 303-348 - Intercepts and routes wallet URIs
